misconceptions.display_name: Uses a constant over an irreducible quadratic
misconceptions.description: Writes A/(x^2 + 1) instead of (B x + C)/(x^2 + 1) for an irreducible quadratic factor. An irreducible quadratic needs a full linear numerator, not a constant.

misconceptions.display_name: Uses one term for a repeated factor
misconceptions.description: For (x - 1)^2 writes only B/(x - 1) and omits the C/(x - 1)^2 term. Each power of a repeated linear factor needs its own term.
trigger_phrases: one term for the squared factor

misconceptions.display_name: Tries partial fractions on an irreducible denominator
misconceptions.description: Attempts to split x^2 + 4 into real linear partial fractions even though it does not factor over the reals. An irreducible quadratic (like x^2 + 4) is an arctan form, not a partial-fractions target.
